finding: CompactionScreen calls call_compaction_api(model_info, prompt_content) with 2 args but the function requires 3 (model, prompt, verbosity) and returns a str, yet the caller does result['content']. TUI compaction would raise TypeError at runtime. Zero test coverage on this headline TUI feature let the bug ship.
proposed_action: Add a TUI compaction test (mock call_compaction_api) asserting it is invoked correctly and a file is written; this both fixes the regression gap and forces the signature fix during execution.

finding: find_turns/extract_opencode_turns (the core recovery parser that turns an opencode export into Turns) has no fixture-based test. It parses external JSON and is the product's headline feature; only downstream helpers (consolidate/truncate) are unit-tested.
disposition: Add a small realistic opencode-export JSON fixture + tests asserting find_turns extracts the expected user/assistant turns (incl. include_tools on/off, and the raw-text fallback).

finding: No test exercises recover_from_export / the CLI recovery+compaction main() flow (export -> parse -> truncate -> render -> write restart/transcript files). The primary CLI use case is unverified end-to-end.
disposition: Add an integration test that feeds a fixture export through the pipeline (mock the opencode subprocess + the API) and asserts the restart/transcript files are produced with expected content.

finding: call_compaction_api (network/LLM client) is untested: HTTPS-enforcement branch, non-HTTPS refusal, HTTP-error envelope, empty-choices/empty-content errors, and success parsing are all unverified.
proposed_action: Add unit tests mocking urllib.request.urlopen: assert non-HTTPS+non-localhost raises, a success payload returns content, and empty/Error responses raise RecoveryError.

finding: render_restart_context, render_compact_prompt, render_transcript produce the user-facing artifacts but have no assertions on their output structure.
column11: Add tests asserting each renderer includes expected headings/turn content for a small Turn list (test-as-documentation of the output contract).

finding: strip_jsonc_comments, parse_json_text, _read_file_ref, expand_env_vars (the opencode-config parsing chain) are largely untested at the ocman.py level (expand_config_refs is tested via the TUI alias only).
proposed_action: Add focused unit tests for JSONC comment stripping, lenient JSON parsing, and {file:}/{env:}/${VAR} expansion incl. malformed input.

finding: estimate_tokens and estimate_cost are pure, trivially testable, and untested.
proposed_action: Add small unit tests pinning token estimate heuristic and cost math (incl. None-cost model returns None).

finding: The legacy db_data.json import fallback branch is exercised only indirectly by the SQLi/traversal rejection tests (which use the old format). A positive old-format round-trip import is not asserted.
proposed_action: Add a positive old-format (db_data.json) import test asserting rows import correctly, to pin that back-compat branch.

finding: CI runs bare pytest, which correctly skips the new OCMAN_BENCHMARK-gated benchmarks; confirm this stays true and document the opt-in run so contributors know it exists.
proposed_action: Document the benchmark opt-in in CONTRIBUTING/README testing section; optionally add a CI note. No CI gate change.

finding: No coverage tooling; hard to see which branches are exercised. Adding coverage gating could be noisy/flaky and force busywork on a large single-file module.
proposed_action: Optionally add coverage as an opt-in local tool (not a CI gate) to guide test authoring; do NOT gate merges on a coverage number.
